methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
BotBuilt is a Seed-stage software platform in Housing & Construction, founded by Barrett Ames and Colin Devine. The company has raised $12.4M to date. The relevant peer universe consists of two private companies operating in construction technology (ICON, Apis Cor), though no public comparables exist in this subsector, and no sufficiently dense peer set of same-stage or adjacent-stage companies met minimum data thresholds for reliable median-based valuation.

Given data constraints, stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($12.4M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to subsequent stages yields an implied valuation of $31M. This reflects the expectation that BotBuilt will command a meaningful markup upon advancement to Series A, consistent with capital-efficiency patterns in early-stage venture funding.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M (low) to $36M (median $31M), with the range reflecting reasonable variance around the stage-progression estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer metrics—precluding sanity-checking against comparable Seed-stage Housing & Construction companies—and lack of granular unit economics or revenue data. Any valuation within this range should be treated as preliminary pending deeper diligence on customer traction, unit CAC, and market sizing in construction software.
